questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: What is the purpose of the slip rings in an AC generator?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
The magnet is heated until current appears.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings maintain contact while allowing the current to alternate as the coil turns.
questions.senNote: Slip rings maintain contact while allowing the current to alternate as the coil turns.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student labels the slip rings on a generator. What is their job?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
Chemical energy directly creates alternating current.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows a rotating coil joined to slip rings. Why are they needed?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
The field lines are destroyed by the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what is the role of slip rings in a simple AC generator?
options: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
A generator works only with plastic coils.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a factory safety note, an apprentice reviews: during generator revision, which part keeps contact with a turning coil?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
The current is caused by friction with air.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a space centre display, a guide explains: a generator output alternates as the coil turns. How do slip rings help this happen?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
The coil becomes radioactive.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: On a revision flashcard, a student practises: the coil spins while connected to an external circuit. What do slip rings allow?
options: The generator stores charge without moving parts.
The coil must stay still in a magnetic field.
There is no energy transfer in a generator.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.
questions.senNote: Slip rings keep the rotating coil connected and allow an alternating output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: What is the shape of the magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire?
options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
The field exists only inside the magnet.
Concentric circles around the wire
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire forms concentric circles.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire forms concentric circles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: compasses are placed around a current-carrying wire. What pattern do they show?
options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
Concentric circles around the wire
A compass points randomly in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.
questions.senNote: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows field lines around a vertical wire. Which shape is correct?
options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Concentric circles around the wire
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
The field is strongest halfway between the poles.
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.
questions.senNote: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: how are magnetic field lines arranged around a straight wire with current?
options: Concentric circles around the wire
Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
The field disappears near the poles.
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.
questions.senNote: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: On a factory safety note, an apprentice reviews: a guide sprinkles iron filings near a live wire. What pattern should appear?
options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
Field lines show the path of electric current.
Concentric circles around the wire
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.
questions.senNote: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: On a space centre display, a guide explains: which diagram would show the magnetic field around a current-carrying wire?
options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.
Field lines point from south to north outside the magnet.
Concentric circles around the wire
Only plastic objects can show field patterns.
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.
questions.senNote: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire

options: Field lines cross each other at the poles.

The field has no direction.
questions.explanation: A straight current-carrying wire has circular magnetic field lines around it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: Which statement about a magnetically soft material is correct?
options: It is hard to magnetise but easy to demagnetise.
It stays magnetised strongly and is best for permanent magnets.
It cannot be used in an electromagnet.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.
questions.senNote: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student chooses a material that magnetises when current flows and demagnetises when it stops. Which property is needed?
options: It cannot be magnetised.
It is always plastic.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It only works when heated red hot.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table compares soft iron and steel. Why pick soft iron for a relay?
options: It blocks all current.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It repels every metal.
It turns into copper.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what does magnetically soft mean for an electromagnet core?
options: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It is always plastic.
It has no domains.
It keeps magnetism permanently.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

options: It repels every metal.

It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a home study card, a learner revises: a log book compares soft iron and steel. Why pick soft iron for a relay?
options: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It only works when heated red hot.
It keeps magnetism permanently.
It is always plastic.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a research log book, an assistant notes: what does magnetically soft mean for an electromagnet core?
options: It turns into copper.
It cannot be magnetised.
It repels every metal.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a planetarium screen, a visitor chooses: an electromagnet must switch off quickly. Why is soft iron suitable?
options: It keeps magnetism permanently.
It blocks all current.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It has no domains.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a radio studio demo, a presenter explains: which statement describes a magnetically soft material?
options: It cannot be magnetised.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It is always plastic.
It only works when heated red hot.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: On a energy survey report, a surveyor writes: why is soft iron useful as the core of an electromagnet?
options: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
It blocks all current.
It repels every metal.
It turns into copper.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.
questions.senNote: Soft iron is easy to magnetise and demagnetise, so it works well in electromagnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: What happens when the north pole of one magnet is brought near the north pole of another magnet?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
A permanent magnet is made only from copper.
They repel each other.
questions.explanation: Like poles repel and unlike poles attract.
questions.senNote: Like poles repel and unlike poles attract.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student pushes like poles together. What happens?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
They repel each other.
A permanent magnet has no north or south pole.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows north facing north. Which result is correct?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
They repel each other.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
It becomes magnetic only when heated.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what happens when the same poles of two magnets are near each other?
options: They repel each other.
A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
Its domains are always completely random.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a factory safety note, an apprentice reviews: a bar magnet's north pole is near another north pole. What should be felt?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
It works because it stores electric charge.
They repel each other.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a space centre display, a guide explains: which prediction is correct for two like magnetic poles?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
They repel each other.
It attracts every material equally.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a revision flashcard, a student practises: two north poles are brought close together. What force acts between them?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
They repel each other.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
It has a field only when touching iron.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a hospital training card, a trainee checks: a astronomer pushes like poles together. What happens?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
They repel each other.
It is the same as an electromagnet.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: On a observatory data sheet, an astronomer reviews: a whiteboard shows north facing north. Which result is correct?
options: A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
They repel each other.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
It cannot repel another magnet.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles

options: They repel each other.
A permanent magnet needs a current all the time.
A permanent magnet loses its field as soon as it is moved.
It needs a battery to keep its field.
questions.explanation: Like magnetic poles repel each other.
questions.senNote: Like magnetic poles repel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use the transformer turns ratio
questions.question: A transformer has 220 turns on the primary coil and 60 turns on the secondary coil. The primary voltage is 140 V. What is the secondary voltage?
questions.explanation: For an ideal transformer, primary voltage divided by secondary voltage equals primary turns divided by secondary turns.
questions.senNote: For an ideal transformer, primary voltage divided by secondary voltage equals primary turns divided by secondary turns.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use the transformer turns ratio
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student uses the turns ratio and gets 46.67 V. What should be recorded?
questions.explanation: The transformer turns-ratio calculation gives 46.67 V.
questions.senNote: The transformer turns-ratio calculation gives 46.67 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use the transformer turns ratio
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: the primary and secondary coil ratio gives an output of 55.38 V. Which value is right?
questions.explanation: The transformer turns-ratio calculation gives 55.38 V.
questions.senNote: The transformer turns-ratio calculation gives 55.38 V.
